title: Camonea umbellata
intro:
I apologize but Camonea umbellata is not found. However, I found that Camellia may be what you are referring to. Camellia umbellata is a plant species in the Camellia genus. Camellia umbellata, also known as the Yunnan camellia, is a species of flowering plant in the tea family (Theaceae). 

Camellia umbellata is a plant species with various health benefits, including its potential to lower cholesterol levels, improve blood lipid profiles, and exhibit antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Camellia umbellata may be attributed to its ability to inhibit the activity of key enzymes involved in cholesterol synthesis and to its high content of polyphenols.

The bioactive constituents of Camellia umbellata include flavonoids, alkaloids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant's flavonoids, including quercetin and kaempferol, have been shown to possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities.

Camellia umbellata is used in traditional Chinese medicine to treat various conditions, including high blood pressure, atherosclerosis, and obesity. The most common herbal preparations made with Camellia umbellata include tea infusions, capsules, and extracts, which are often standardized to contain specific levels of polyphenols and other bioactive compounds.
